message:
feat(error): add NotImplemented variant for graceful stub handling

Add MsError::NotImplemented variant to the error enum for functions
that are stubbed out but may be called at runtime.

This replaces the use of todo!() macro which causes immediate panic.
Using a proper error variant provides:

1. Graceful error handling - callers can catch and handle the error
2. Clear messaging - "Not implemented: <description>" format
3. No panics - returns Err() instead of crashing
4. Consistent error typing - integrates with Result<T, MsError>

Applied to src/updater/download.rs:
  - download() now returns Err(MsError::NotImplemented(...))
    instead of todo!("download not implemented")

This pattern should be used for all stub functions that may be
invoked before implementation is complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
